channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇹🇼 China Labels Taiwan President a Rat After Failed Diplomatic Blockade
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te arrived in Eswatini for a surprise visit after China pressured multiple African nations to deny overflight permits. Beijing condemned Lai as a rat, highlighting escalating cross-strait tensions.

item.title: 🚢 US Warns Shippers of Sanctions for Paying Iran Strait of Hormuz Tolls
item.description: The United States has warned global shipping companies they could face sanctions if they pay Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the financial dimension of the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Trump Auto Tariffs Could Cost Germany Nearly $18 Billion in Output
item.description: A German economic institute estimates President Trumps tariff hike on EU cars and trucks could cost Germany nearly 15 billion euros in lost output, straining transatlantic trade relations.

item.title: 🛢️ Iraq Says Oil Output Can Recover Within Week Once Hormuz Crisis Ends
item.description: Deputy Oil Minister Basim Mohammed stated Iraq can restore oil output and exports to normal levels within seven days of the Strait of Hormuz crisis concluding, as the country seeks alternative export routes.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan to Receive Russian Sakhalin-2 Oil Cargo as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
item.description: Japanese refinery Taiyo Oil will receive a crude oil cargo from Russias Sakhalin-2 project next week, as Japan seeks alternative petroleum sources following the US-Iran conflict that limited Gulf supplies.

item.title: 🇰🇭 Cambodia Upholds Treason Conviction of Opposition Leader
item.description: A Cambodian appeals court upheld the treason conviction and 27-year prison sentence for opposition leader Kem Sokha, drawing concern from the US State Department and UN rights chief.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran War Reaches Two-Month Mark With No Clear Victory
item.description: Two months into the US-Iran conflict, fighting has been suspended but a definitive end remains elusive. Washington lacks clear strategic gains as oil prices stay elevated and diplomatic solutions stall.

item.title: 💰 Iran War Drives US Inflation Expectations Higher
item.description: US headline and core inflation are heading back above 3% as the Iran war-related oil shock continues to affect prices, building inflation expectations for the year ahead.

item.title: ⚓ US Navy Turns to AI Firm to Counter Iranian Mines in Strait
item.description: The US Navy has contracted AI company Domino to develop countermeasures against Iranian naval mines as the Strait of Hormuz crisis continues. The move reflects growing concerns about Iranian mining capabilities disrupting global oil shipments.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Develops SQUID Technology to Detect US Nuclear Submarines
item.description: Chinese scientists have made advances in gravity-detecting SQUID technology that could make it easier to track US nuclear submarines. The development could shift the strategic balance in underwater warfare.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Makes Surprise Visit to Eswatini After China Cancels Trip
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te visited Eswatini days after China forced cancellation of his original trip. China called the visit a "stowaway-style escape farce." The diplomatic incident highlights ongoing tensions between Taiwan and China over international recognition.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Condemns New US Sanctions as "Collective Punishment"
item.description: Cuba denounced fresh US sanctions as illegal and abusive, coming on top of a US oil blockade causing widespread blackouts and fuel shortages. The new measures escalate economic pressure on the island nation.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Investigates Soldiers for Role in Military Base Attacks
item.description: Five army personnel including three active-duty soldiers identified as suspects in last week coordinated attacks on military bases. The investigation raises concerns about insider threats amid ongoing security challenges.

item.title: 🇿🇲 Zambia Cancels World Largest Human Rights Summit Days Before Start
item.description: Government blocked RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not align with national values. The cancellation of the world largest human rights and tech summit raises concerns about democratic freedoms.

item.title: 🏥 First Malaria Drug for Babies Approved in Major Public Health Milestone
item.description: A new malaria drug specifically for babies has been approved, marking significant progress in fighting a disease that kills hundreds of thousands of children annually in Africa.

item.title: 🚢⚠️ Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Yemen, Second in 10 Days as Somali Piracy Surges
item.description: Somali pirates seized MT Eureka in the Gulf of Aden, the fourth successful hijacking in two weeks. The surge in piracy follows international navies redirecting to counter Houthi threats in the Red Sea.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US delays arms shipments to Europe, potentially affecting Ukraine
item.description: Washington has warned European allies to expect long delays in US weapons deliveries, including HIMARS and NASAMS munitions, as the US replenishes stocks depleted during the Iran war. The delays could affect Ukraine's air defense capabilities.

item.title: 🇨🇿 Czechia allows Fico Moscow flight as neighbors close airspace
item.description: Czechia granted Slovakia permission to use its airspace for Robert Fico flight to Moscow for Victory Day, while Baltic states and Poland denied access. Fico plans to attend Russia May 9 military parade despite EU criticism.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky invites Fico to Kyiv ahead of Moscow Victory Day trip
item.description: President Zelensky called Slovak PM Robert Fico and invited him to Kyiv before Fico planned visit to Moscow for May 9 Victory Day. Despite tensions, both leaders emphasized the need for strong relations and Slovakia support for Ukraine EU membership.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru investigates citizens trafficked to fight for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: Peru launched an investigation after relatives reported citizens were deceived with fake job offers and financial promises, then forcibly sent to fight in Russia war against Ukraine once in Russia.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian proposal would open Strait before nuclear talks, Trump rejected it
item.description: Details emerged of Iran's proposal to end the war: Iran would reopen the Strait of Hormuz before entering nuclear negotiations. Trump rejected the offer, saying he was "not satisfied" but still prefers a non-military resolution.

item.title: ✈️ Spirit Airlines to cease operations overnight, preparing for shutdown
item.description: Bankrupt US discount carrier Spirit Airlines is preparing to cease operations around 3 a.m. Saturday, marking the collapse of the ultra-low-cost carrier model. The airline had sought government bailout assistance that did not materialize.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran standoff could leave Trump worse off than before war
item.description: Analysis finds the US-Iran conflict has failed to deliver a decisive military or diplomatic victory. With the war's stated goals unmet and regional tensions elevated, Trump may face a worse strategic position than before the conflict began.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskiy discusses Ukraine EU accession with Slovakia PM Fico
item.description: Ukraine's President Zelenskiy held talks with Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico about Ukraine's path to European Union membership. Fico has been skeptical of Ukraine support, making the diplomatic engagement significant.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸 Germany: US Troop Withdrawal Should Spur European Defense Independence
item.description: Germany's defense minister said the Pentagon's decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany underlines Europe's need to take greater responsibility for its own defense, as tensions over the Iran war and tariffs strain US-European relations.
